design_philosophy.core_loop: High-speed pursuit driving with time pressure management: reach fleeing criminal within time limit, ram vehicle multiple times to force stop, arrest criminal before escape
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. vehicle control: player must balance speed with accuracy to navigate road splits correctly while maintaining pursuit before criminal escapes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Police officer in high-speed chase, operating advanced police vehicle (Porsche 928) to apprehend dangerous criminals across varied environments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: First-person perspective adaptation (unlike arcade original's third-person view); shifts from arcade's objective-based chase mechanic to something more immersive and driving-focused for home console audience

difficulty.continues_system: Game uses time extensions upon reaching criminal vehicle; repeated crashes/slow driving results in criminal escape and level failure
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based structure with 5 distinct chase scenarios; no mid-level checkpoints; level failure results in restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select code available via cheat codes (PAR codes), suggesting developer allowance for difficulty adjustment through sequence selection

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression based on successful criminal apprehension; 5 levels total with escalating difficulty
replayability_hooks: Scoring system with bonuses for completing without continues (100,000 × level) and time bonuses (5,000 per second saved)
5,000,000-point bonus award for beating entire game encourages replay for high scores
Multiple road splits per level create variable routes, suggesting different optimal paths for speedrunning

world_building_techniques: Radio communications from Nancy at Chase HQ at level start providing criminal briefing
Criminal character archetypes (e.g., 'Idaho Slasher', 'New York armed robber', 'L.A. kidnapper') establish setting context
Authentic radio frequency references (144-148 MHz ham radio band) add verisimilitude to police procedural framing

notable_bosses: Ralph, the Idaho Slasher (White Lotus Esprit)
Carlos, the New York armed robber (Yellow Lamborghini Countach)
Eastern Bloc Spy (Red Porsche 928) - final level, vehicle always listed as 'unidentified'

faction_indicators: Law enforcement player identity (police officer Tony Gibson, Chase H.Q. department)
Criminal archetypes organized by geography (Idaho, New York, Chicago, L.A., Eastern Bloc)
danger_signals: Escalating criminal threat levels across 5-level arc (from local perpetrators to international spy)
Vehicle-based combat framing (ramming mechanics) emphasizes physical confrontation
environmental_storytelling: Regional criminal assignments suggest distributed crime network across U.S. and international borders
Vehicle selection (Porsche 928, Ferrari, Lamborghini, Lotus) reflects criminal sophistication and resource levels

sources_used: tcrf - Super Chase H.Q. (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
